---
title: "Group Reflection"
---

## Skills inventory

For each skill area, describe what your group actually used it for.
If you did not use a skill, say so and note why.

### Functions & refactoring (W2)

*What we did:* function for plotting sample size distributions

*What worked well:* using the function in the code once it was written

*What was hard:* had to do some research on stack overflow on how to pass the 
name of a variable to a function (eval(sym()))

### Debugging (W3)

*What we did:* Look at error messages and - if code ran without errors - look
at outputs to check plausability

*What worked well:* First thinking about it myself, then using google and - if 
nothing else worked - ask AI

*What was hard:*

### Version control & remotes (W4)

*What we did:* worked on separate branches

*What worked well:* barely any merge conflicts and no nasty ones

*What was hard:* keeping commits small

### Quarto websites & collaborative coding (W5)

*What we did:* peer-reviews, assisting each other where necessary, merging each
others branches

*What worked well:* distributing tasks about equally - I feel like we both 
contributed about the same

*What was hard:* time management - we both did our task *very* close to respective
deadlines.

### R packages (W6)

*What we did:* use imports as to not code pre-existing functionality

*What worked well:* rather clean code

*What was hard:* we had no formal package management but since we did not use
that many packages I feel like that is okay, also most packages were standard so 
when people pull our project most users should not experience too many problems

### Initial data analysis & data cleaning (W7)

*What we did:* looking at missing values, creating a new column for the date

*What worked well:* looking at the documentation and inferring why values might be
missing

*What was hard:* getting a total overview over all indicators the data includes
as it is very comprehensive but not structured to capture that at a quick glance

### Reproducibility, open data & renv (W9)

*What we did:* sharing our data and linking the source, documenting code

*What worked well:* doing all analyses in the scripts for reproducibility (not)
in consoles

*What was hard:*

### Analysis & inference strategies (W10)

*What we did:* Correlation and regression analyses for various groups and models

*What worked well:* using Claude for parts of the analysis (as explained in the
code), comparing different models.

*What was hard:* Inferring which models worked best and why, interpreting the
results

### Communication & visualisation (W11)

*What we did:* Visualizing sample sizes

*What worked well:* Asking AI for help on visualizations, including explanations
for what the parameters are doing

*What was hard:*

## Key analytical decisions

Describe 2–3 decisions that had a meaningful effect on your results or workflow.

### Decision 1: [short title]

*What were we deciding between?*

*How did we reason through it?*

*In hindsight, would we decide differently?*

### Decision 2: [short title]

*What were we deciding between?*

*How did we reason through it?*

*In hindsight, would we decide differently?*

## Workflow & collaboration

*How did we divide work across group members?*

*What branching or Git workflow did we actually use — and did it match the proposal?*

*What would we change about how we coordinated?* Maybe not completing everything
on the day of the deadline (typing this on the day of the deadline)

## LLM usage

Describe up to 5 significant uses of an LLM tool during the project.

### Usage 1: [task]

- **Tool / model:**
- **What it produced:**
- **What we had to correct or verify:**

### Usage 2: [task]

- **Tool / model:**
- **What it produced:**
- **What we had to correct or verify:**
